question: In the wilderness of which place did God speak to Moses at the beginning of the book of Numbers?
explanation: Numbers 1:1 states that the Lord spoke to Moses in the Wilderness of Sinai in the Tabernacle of the Congregation.

question: What was the minimum age for men to be numbered for war in Israel?
explanation: According to Numbers 1:3, all that are able to go forth to war in Israel from twenty years old and upward were to be numbered.

question: Who was the head of the house of his fathers for the tribe of Reuben?
explanation: Numbers 1:5 names Elizur the son of Shedeur as the representative for Reuben.

question: Which tribe was Nahshon the son of Amminadab representing?
explanation: In Numbers 1:7, Nahshon the son of Amminadab is listed as the leader for the tribe of Judah.

question: Which tribe was NOT numbered among the children of Israel in this census?
explanation: Numbers 1:47 states that the Levites after the tribe of their fathers were not numbered among them.

question: What was the total number of the children of Israel able to go to war, excluding the Levites?
explanation: Numbers 1:46 records the total sum as six hundred thousand and three thousand and five hundred and fifty.

question: What was the specific responsibility given to the Levites regarding the Tabernacle?
opt2: To lead the army into battle
opt4: To keep charge of the Tabernacle and its vessels
correct: To keep charge of the Tabernacle and its vessels
explanation: Numbers 1:50 states the Levites were appointed over the tabernacle of testimony and over all the vessels thereof.

question: Where were the children of Israel commanded to pitch their tents?
opt1: Every man by his own standard
opt2: All together in one large circle
correct: Every man by his own standard
explanation: Numbers 1:52 says every man shall pitch by his own standard, throughout their hosts.

question: Who was the leader appointed for the tribe of Dan?
explanation: Numbers 1:12 lists Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai for the tribe of Dan.

question: How many men were numbered in the tribe of Judah?
explanation: Numbers 1:27 states those that were numbered of the tribe of Judah were threescore and fourteen thousand and six hundred.

question: What was the consequence for a 'stranger' who came near the Tabernacle while it was being moved?
opt2: He shall be put to death
correct: He shall be put to death
explanation: Numbers 1:51 states that when the tabernacle is to be pitched, the Levites shall set it up: and the stranger that cometh nigh shall be put to death.

question: Who was the leader for the tribe of Benjamin?
explanation: Numbers 1:11 identifies Abidan the son of Gideoni for the tribe of Benjamin.

question: Which tribe had 46,500 men numbered?
explanation: Numbers 1:21 says the tribe of Reuben numbered forty and six thousand and five hundred.

question: Where did the Levites pitch their tents in relation to the Tabernacle?
opt2: Round about the tabernacle of testimony
opt3: At the entrance of the camp
correct: Round about the tabernacle of testimony
explanation: Numbers 1:53 states the Levites shall pitch round about the tabernacle of testimony.

question: Who assisted Moses and Aaron in numbering the people?
explanation: Numbers 1:4 says 'And with you there shall be a man of every tribe; every one head of the house of his fathers.'

question: Which tribe was commanded to pitch on the East side toward the rising sun?
explanation: Numbers 2:3 states that on the east side toward the rising of the sun shall they of the standard of the camp of Judah pitch.

question: Which two tribes were to pitch next to Judah on the East side?
explanation: Numbers 2:5-7 lists Issachar and Zebulun as pitching next to Judah.

question: Which tribe was the leader of the camp on the South side?
explanation: Numbers 2:10 states the standard of the camp of Reuben shall be on the south side.

question: What was located in the midst of the camps?
explanation: Numbers 2:17 says the tabernacle of the congregation shall set forward with the camp of the Levites in the midst of the camp.

question: Which tribe led the camp on the West side?
explanation: Numbers 2:18 states the standard of the camp of Ephraim shall be on the west side.

question: Which tribe led the camp on the North side?
explanation: Numbers 2:25 states the standard of the camp of Dan shall be on the north side.

question: In what order were the tribes to set forward (march)?
explanation: Numbers 2:17 states they shall set forward 'as they encamp, every man in his place by their standards.'

question: How many tribes in total were organized into the four main camps?
explanation: Chapters 1 and 2 list the 12 tribes (excluding Levi, but counting Ephraim and Manasseh as separate) organized into four divisions.

question: Which tribe was mentioned as being between Ephraim and Benjamin on the West?
explanation: Numbers 2:20 mentions the tribe of Manasseh as pitching by the tribe of Ephraim.

question: Who was the captain of the children of Issachar?
explanation: Numbers 2:5 names Nethaneel the son of Zuar as the captain of Issachar.

question: Which tribe had the largest number in their camp according to Chapter 2?
explanation: The camp of Judah (including Issachar and Zebulun) totaled 186,400 (Numbers 2:9), making it the largest.

question: Who was the captain for the tribe of Gad?
explanation: Numbers 2:14 mentions Eliasaph the son of Reuel as the captain of Gad.

question: How many were numbered in the camp of Ephraim?
explanation: Numbers 2:24 states the total for the camp of Ephraim was 108,100.

question: Which tribe pitched next to Dan on the North side?
explanation: Numbers 2:27 states that those that encamp by Dan shall be the tribe of Asher.

question: Who was the captain of the tribe of Naphtali?
explanation: Numbers 2:29 names Ahira the son of Enan as the captain of Naphtali.

question: Who were the four sons of Aaron?
explanation: Numbers 3:2 lists the names of the sons of Aaron: Nadab the firstborn, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

question: What happened to Nadab and Abihu?
opt1: They were promoted to high priests
opt2: They died before the Lord for offering strange fire
opt3: They led a rebellion against Moses
opt4: They entered the Promised Land early
correct: They died before the Lord for offering strange fire
explanation: Numbers 3:4 says Nadab and Abihu died before the Lord when they offered strange fire.

question: Who did the Lord take instead of the firstborn of the children of Israel?
explanation: Numbers 3:12 says 'And I, behold, I have taken the Levites from among the children of Israel instead of all the firstborn.'

question: At what age were the males of the Levites to be numbered?
opt3: From a month old and upward
correct: From a month old and upward
explanation: Numbers 3:15 commands to number the children of Levi 'every male from a month old and upward.'

question: What were the three families of Levi?
explanation: Numbers 3:17 names the sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.

question: Where did the family of the Gershonites pitch their tents?
opt2: On the West side behind the tabernacle
correct: On the West side behind the tabernacle
explanation: Numbers 3:23 states the families of the Gershonites shall pitch behind the tabernacle westward.

question: What was the charge of the sons of Gershon?
opt2: The boards and bars of the tabernacle
opt3: The tabernacle, the tent, and the coverings
correct: The tabernacle, the tent, and the coverings
explanation: Numbers 3:25-26 describes the charge of the sons of Gershon as the tabernacle, the tent, the covering, and the hanging for the door.

question: Where did the families of the sons of Kohath pitch?
explanation: Numbers 3:29 states the families of the sons of Kohath shall pitch on the side of the tabernacle southward.

question: What was the charge of the sons of Kohath?
opt2: The ark, table, candlestick, and altars
correct: The ark, table, candlestick, and altars
explanation: Numbers 3:31 lists their charge as the ark, the table, the candlestick, the altars, and the vessels of the sanctuary.

question: Who was the chief over the chief of the Levites?
explanation: Numbers 3:32 states Eleazar the son of Aaron shall be chief over the chief of the Levites.

question: Where did the family of Merari pitch?
explanation: Numbers 3:35 states the families of Merari shall pitch on the side of the tabernacle northward.

question: Who pitched on the East side of the tabernacle, before the entrance?
explanation: Numbers 3:38 states that Moses, Aaron, and his sons pitched on the east, before the tabernacle of the congregation.

question: What was the total number of Levites numbered by Moses and Aaron?
explanation: Numbers 3:39 states the total number of Levites was twenty and two thousand.

question: When the firstborn males of Israel were numbered, how many more were there than the Levites?
explanation: Numbers 3:46 mentions the two hundred and threescore and thirteen (273) of the firstborn which are more than the Levites.

question: What was the redemption price for each of the extra firstborn males?
explanation: Numbers 3:47 says 'Thou shalt even take five shekels apiece by the poll' for the redemption of the extra firstborn.

question: What was the age range for the Levites who were numbered to do the work in the tabernacle of the congregation?
explanation: Numbers 4:3 specifies the age as 'from thirty years old and upward even until fifty years old'.

question: Which family of the Levites was responsible for carrying the most holy things, such as the ark?
explanation: Numbers 4:4-15 describes the service of the sons of Kohath regarding the most holy things.

question: What was used to cover the ark of testimony before it was moved?
explanation: Numbers 4:5 states that Aaron and his sons shall take down the covering veil and cover the ark of testimony with it.

question: What color was the cloth spread over the table of shewbread?
explanation: Numbers 4:8 says they shall spread upon them a cloth of scarlet.

question: What was the specific instruction to the sons of Kohath regarding the holy things to avoid death?
opt1: They must not touch any holy thing
opt2: They must not look at the ark
correct: They must not touch any holy thing
explanation: Numbers 4:15 warns that the sons of Kohath shall come to bear it, but they shall not touch any holy thing, lest they die.

question: Who was the oversight of the oil for the light and the sweet incense committed to?
explanation: Numbers 4:16 appoints Eleazar the son of Aaron to the oversight of the oil, incense, and daily meat offering.

question: Under whose hand was the service of the families of the Gershonites?
explanation: Numbers 4:28 states their service was under the hand of Ithamar the son of Aaron.

question: Which family was responsible for the boards, bars, pillars, and sockets of the tabernacle?
explanation: Numbers 4:31-32 assigns the boards, bars, pillars, and sockets to the sons of Merari.

question: How many men were numbered of the sons of Kohath (ages 30-50)?
explanation: Numbers 4:36 records the number of the families of the Kohathites as 2,750.

question: How many men were numbered of the sons of Gershon (ages 30-50)?
explanation: Numbers 4:40 records the number of the sons of Gershon as 2,630.

question: How many men were numbered of the sons of Merari (ages 30-50)?
explanation: Numbers 4:44 records the number of the families of the sons of Merari as 3,200.

question: What was the total number of Levites aged 30 to 50 who were numbered for service?
explanation: Numbers 4:48 says the total numbered was eight thousand and five hundred and fourscore (8,580).

question: What kind of skins were used as the outermost covering for the tabernacle items during travel?
explanation: Multiple verses in Numbers 4 (v. 6, 8, 10, 11, 12, 14) mention the use of badgers' skins as a covering.

question: What must the Kohathites NOT do when the holy things are being covered, to avoid death?
opt1: Go in to see when the holy things are covered
correct: Go in to see when the holy things are covered
explanation: Numbers 4:20 says 'But they shall not go in to see when the holy things are covered, lest they die.'

question: Who was tasked with assigning each man of the Merarites his specific burden?
opt4: By name, according to the appointment of Moses
correct: By name, according to the appointment of Moses
explanation: Numbers 4:32 says 'and by name ye shall reckon the instruments of the charge of their burden'.

question: Who did God command Moses to put out of the camp to avoid defiling it?
opt1: Every leper, everyone with an issue, and anyone defiled by the dead
opt4: People from the tribe of Dan
correct: Every leper, everyone with an issue, and anyone defiled by the dead
explanation: Numbers 5:2-3 commands to put out every leper, every one that hath an issue, and whosoever is defiled by the dead.

question: When a person commits a sin against another, what must they do besides confessing?
opt2: Recompense the trespass with the principal and add a fifth part
correct: Recompense the trespass with the principal and add a fifth part
explanation: Numbers 5:7 states the person shall recompense his trespass with the principal thereof, and add unto it the fifth part.

question: If a person has no kinsman to receive the restitution, to whom does the restitution go?
opt3: To the Lord, even to the priest
correct: To the Lord, even to the priest
explanation: Numbers 5:8 says the trespass shall be recompensed unto the Lord, even to the priest.

question: What is the name of the law concerning a husband who suspects his wife of unfaithfulness?
explanation: Numbers 5:29 calls this specific ritual 'the law of jealousies'.

question: What offering did the husband bring for his wife in the trial of jealousy?
opt2: Tenth part of an ephah of barley meal
opt4: Fine flour with oil and frankincense
correct: Tenth part of an ephah of barley meal
explanation: Numbers 5:15 says he shall bring the tenth part of an ephah of barley meal.

question: What was NOT added to the offering of jealousy?
explanation: Numbers 5:15 states 'he shall pour no oil upon it, nor put frankincense thereon; for it is an offering of jealousy'.

question: What kind of water was used in the trial of jealousy?
opt2: Holy water in an earthen vessel
correct: Holy water in an earthen vessel
explanation: Numbers 5:17 states the priest shall take holy water in an earthen vessel.

question: What was mixed into the holy water during the trial?
opt2: Dust from the floor of the tabernacle
correct: Dust from the floor of the tabernacle
explanation: Numbers 5:17 says the priest shall take of the dust that is in the floor of the tabernacle and put it into the water.

question: What did the priest do with the written curses in the trial?
opt2: Blotted them out with the bitter water
correct: Blotted them out with the bitter water
explanation: Numbers 5:23 says 'the priest shall write these curses in a book, and he shall blot them out with the bitter water'.

question: What would happen to the woman if she were guilty after drinking the water?
opt2: Her belly would swell and her thigh would rot
correct: Her belly would swell and her thigh would rot
explanation: Numbers 5:27 says 'her belly shall swell, and her thigh shall rot: and the woman shall be a curse among her people'.

question: What would happen to the woman if she were innocent after drinking the water?
opt2: She would be free and shall conceive seed
correct: She would be free and shall conceive seed
explanation: Numbers 5:28 states if the woman be not defiled, but be clean, then she shall be free, and shall conceive seed.

question: Who stands before the Lord during the trial of jealousy?
explanation: Numbers 5:16 and 5:18 mention the priest bringing the woman and setting her before the Lord.

question: What was the purpose of putting out the defiled from the camp?
opt2: That they defile not their camps, in the midst whereof God dwells
opt4: To prevent the spread of war
correct: That they defile not their camps, in the midst whereof God dwells
explanation: Numbers 5:3 explains the reason is so they don't defile the camp where God dwells.

question: What response did the woman give after the priest pronounced the curse?
explanation: Numbers 5:22 states 'And the woman shall say, Amen, amen'.

question: Why is the offering called 'an offering of memorial'?
explanation: Numbers 5:15 describes it as an offering of memorial, bringing iniquity to remembrance.

question: What was the name for a man or woman who took a special vow to separate themselves unto the Lord?
explanation: Numbers 6:2 mentions the vow of a Nazarite, to separate themselves unto the Lord.

question: What was a Nazarite forbidden to drink or eat from the grapevine?
opt2: Anything that is made of the vine tree, from the kernels to the husk
correct: Anything that is made of the vine tree, from the kernels to the husk
explanation: Numbers 6:3-4 lists wine, vinegar, grapes, raisins, and everything from the vine as forbidden.

question: What was the rule regarding the hair of a Nazarite during the vow?
opt1: It must be cut every week
opt2: There shall no razor come upon his head
correct: There shall no razor come upon his head
explanation: Numbers 6:5 states 'All the days of the vow of his separation there shall no razor come upon his head'.

question: A Nazarite was forbidden to come near what during the days of separation?
explanation: Numbers 6:6 says 'he shall come at no dead body'.

question: What happened if someone died suddenly beside a Nazarite?
opt2: He had to shave his head and start the days over
opt4: He had to pay a fine
correct: He had to shave his head and start the days over
explanation: Numbers 6:9-12 explains that he must shave his head on the seventh day and the previous days shall be lost.

question: On which day did a Nazarite who was defiled by a dead body have to shave his head?
explanation: Numbers 6:9 states 'he shall shave his head in the day of his cleansing, on the seventh day'.

question: What was a Nazarite to do with his hair when the days of separation were fulfilled?
opt2: Shave it and put it in the fire under the peace offering
correct: Shave it and put it in the fire under the peace offering
explanation: Numbers 6:18 says the Nazarite shall shave the head and put the hair in the fire which is under the sacrifice of the peace offerings.

question: To whom did God give the specific words for blessing the children of Israel?
opt1: Moses to speak to Aaron and his sons
correct: Moses to speak to Aaron and his sons
explanation: Numbers 6:22-23 says 'And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying, Speak unto Aaron and unto his sons...'

question: The Lord bless thee, and _____ thee (Numbers 6:24):
explanation: Numbers 6:24 begins the blessing with 'The Lord bless thee, and keep thee'.

question: What is requested for the Lord to lift up upon the person in the priestly blessing?
explanation: Numbers 6:26 says 'The Lord lift up his countenance upon thee'.

question: What is the final word promised in the priestly blessing?
explanation: Numbers 6:26 ends with 'and give thee peace'.

question: What did the Lord say the priests would put upon the children of Israel by using this blessing?
explanation: Numbers 6:27 states 'And they shall put my name upon the children of Israel; and I will bless them'.

question: What offering did the Nazarite bring for a sin offering at the end of his separation?
explanation: Numbers 6:14 mentions 'one ewe lamb of the first year without blemish for a sin offering'.

question: Could a Nazarite eat bread with leaven during the completion of the vow ceremony?
explanation: Numbers 6:15 specifies a basket of unleavened bread.

question: After all the sacrifices and the shaving of the head, what was the Nazarite then allowed to do?
explanation: Numbers 6:20 states 'and after that the Nazarite may drink wine'.

question: What did the princes of Israel offer when the tabernacle was fully set up and anointed?
opt2: Six covered wagons and twelve oxen
correct: Six covered wagons and twelve oxen
explanation: Numbers 7:3 states they brought six covered wagons and twelve oxen before the Lord.

question: To which group of Levites did Moses give no wagons or oxen?
explanation: Numbers 7:9 says 'But unto the sons of Kohath he gave none: because the service of the sanctuary belonging unto them was that they should bear upon their shoulders.'

question: Over how many days did the princes offer their dedication offerings for the altar?
explanation: Numbers 7 describes the offering of one prince per day for twelve days.

question: Who was the first prince to offer his offering on the first day?
explanation: Numbers 7:12 identifies Nahshon of the tribe of Judah as the first to offer.

question: What was the weight of the silver charger (plate) offered by each prince?
explanation: Numbers 7:13 states the silver charger weighed an hundred and thirty shekels.

question: What was inside the golden spoon of ten shekels offered by the princes?
explanation: Numbers 7:14 mentions 'One golden spoon of ten shekels, full of incense'.

question: How did God speak to Moses after the tabernacle was dedicated?
opt2: From between the two cherubims upon the ark
correct: From between the two cherubims upon the ark
explanation: Numbers 7:89 says Moses heard the voice speaking from off the mercy seat from between the two cherubims.

question: Which prince offered on the seventh day?
explanation: Numbers 7:48 names Elishama of the tribe of Ephraim for the seventh day.

question: What animal was NOT part of the sacrifice for a peace offering from each prince?
explanation: Numbers 7:17 lists two oxen, five rams, five he goats, and five lambs, but not pigeons.

question: What was the total number of golden spoons offered by all twelve princes?
explanation: Numbers 7:86 states there were twelve golden spoons, full of incense.

question: Who was the prince of the tribe of Gad that offered on the sixth day?
explanation: Numbers 7:42 identifies Eliasaph the son of Deuel for the tribe of Gad.

question: What was the total weight of all the silver from the chargers and bowls?
explanation: Numbers 7:85 states all the silver vessels weighed two thousand and four hundred shekels.

question: How many lambs of the first year were offered in total for the burnt offering?
explanation: Numbers 7:87 says there were twelve lambs of the first year for the burnt offering.

question: To whom did Moses give four wagons and eight oxen?
explanation: Numbers 7:8 states four wagons and eight oxen he gave unto the sons of Merari.

question: The silver vessels were weighed after the shekel of what?
explanation: Numbers 7:13 and following verses specify 'after the shekel of the sanctuary'.

question: How many lamps was Aaron commanded to light to give light over against the candlestick?
explanation: Numbers 8:2 says 'the seven lamps shall give light over against the candlestick'.

question: What material was the candlestick made of?
explanation: Numbers 8:4 says 'And this work of the candlestick was of beaten gold'.

question: What was the first step in cleansing the Levites?
opt1: Sprinkle water of purifying upon them
correct: Sprinkle water of purifying upon them
explanation: Numbers 8:7 begins the cleansing by sprinkling water of purifying upon them.

question: What must the Levites do to their bodies as part of the cleansing process?
explanation: Numbers 8:7 says 'let them shave all their flesh, and let them wash their clothes'.

question: Who laid their hands upon the Levites during their dedication?
explanation: Numbers 8:10 states 'the children of Israel shall put their hands upon the Levites'.

question: Aaron offered the Levites before the Lord as what kind of offering?
explanation: Numbers 8:11 says 'And Aaron shall offer the Levites before the Lord for an offering (wave offering) of the children of Israel'.

question: Why did God claim the Levites as His own?
opt2: Instead of the firstborn of all the children of Israel
correct: Instead of the firstborn of all the children of Israel
explanation: Numbers 8:16-17 explains they are taken instead of the firstborn whom God sanctified on the day He smote the firstborn of Egypt.

question: At what age were the Levites to begin their service in the tabernacle?
explanation: Numbers 8:24 states 'from twenty and five years old and upward they shall go in to wait upon the service'.

question: At what age were the Levites required to retire from the oversight of the work?
explanation: Numbers 8:25 says 'And from the age of fifty years they shall cease waiting upon the service'.

question: What could a Levite do after retiring at age 50?
opt2: Minister with their brethren to keep the charge, but do no service
correct: Minister with their brethren to keep the charge, but do no service
explanation: Numbers 8:26 says they shall minister with their brethren to keep the charge, and shall do no service.

question: Who performed the atonement for the Levites to cleanse them?
explanation: Numbers 8:21 states 'and Aaron made an atonement for them to cleanse them'.

question: What was the purpose of the Levites' service?
opt1: To do the service of the children of Israel and make atonement for them
correct: To do the service of the children of Israel and make atonement for them
explanation: Numbers 8:19 explains their purpose is to do the service and prevent plagues among the people.

question: How many bullocks were used for the Levites' sacrifice?
explanation: Numbers 8:12 mentions two bullocks: one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering.

question: Was the candlestick made according to a specific pattern?
opt2: Yes, according to the pattern the Lord showed Moses
opt4: It was found in the desert
correct: Yes, according to the pattern the Lord showed Moses
explanation: Numbers 8:4 says 'according unto the pattern which the Lord had shewed Moses'.

question: What did the Levites wash along with themselves?
explanation: Numbers 8:21 says 'And the Levites were purified, and they washed their clothes'.

question: In which month were the children of Israel commanded to keep the Passover in the wilderness of Sinai?
explanation: Numbers 9:1-2 states they were to keep the Passover in the first month of the second year.

question: On what day of the month was the Passover to be kept?
explanation: Numbers 9:3 says 'In the fourteenth day of this month, at even, ye shall keep it'.

question: What problem did some men have that prevented them from keeping the Passover?
opt2: They were defiled by a dead body
correct: They were defiled by a dead body
explanation: Numbers 9:6 mentions certain men who were defiled by the dead body of a man.

question: What did Moses do when he didn't know the answer to the men's problem?
opt3: He told them to wait while he heard what the Lord would command
opt4: He told them they couldn't participate
correct: He told them to wait while he heard what the Lord would command
explanation: Numbers 9:8: 'Stand still, and I will hear what the Lord will command concerning you'.

question: When was the 'Second Passover' allowed for those who were unclean or on a journey?
opt2: The fourteenth day of the second month
opt3: The first day of the next year
correct: The fourteenth day of the second month
explanation: Numbers 9:10-11 allows a second chance in the second month for those unable to keep the first.

question: With what two things were they commanded to eat the Passover lamb?
explanation: Numbers 9:11: 'eat it with unleavened bread and bitter herbs'.

question: What rule was given regarding the bones of the Passover lamb?
opt2: They shall not break any bone of it
opt4: They must be given to dogs
correct: They shall not break any bone of it
explanation: Numbers 9:12 says 'nor break any bone of it'.

question: What was the punishment for a person who was clean and NOT on a journey but neglected to keep the Passover?
opt2: That soul shall be cut off from among his people
correct: That soul shall be cut off from among his people
explanation: Numbers 9:13 states that the man who neglects the Passover 'even the same soul shall be cut off from among his people'.

question: Could a stranger (foreigner) keep the Passover?
opt2: Yes, if he keeps it according to the ordinance
opt3: Only if he was a servant
correct: Yes, if he keeps it according to the ordinance
explanation: Numbers 9:14 says 'And if a stranger shall sojourn among you... so shall he do'.

question: What covered the tabernacle on the day it was reared up?
explanation: Numbers 9:15: 'the cloud covered the tabernacle'.

question: What did the cloud look like over the tabernacle at night?
explanation: Numbers 9:15-16 says 'at even there was upon the tabernacle as it were the appearance of fire, until the morning'.

question: When were the children of Israel supposed to journey?
opt2: When the cloud was taken up from the tabernacle
correct: When the cloud was taken up from the tabernacle
explanation: Numbers 9:17: 'And when the cloud was taken up from the tabernacle, then after that the children of Israel journeyed'.

question: How long did the children of Israel stay in one place?
opt3: As long as the cloud abode upon the tabernacle
opt4: Until they ran out of water
correct: As long as the cloud abode upon the tabernacle
explanation: Numbers 9:18-22 explains they stayed whether it was two days, a month, or a year, as long as the cloud stayed.

question: Who decided when the camp would rest and when it would move?
opt3: The Lord (by the commandment of the Lord)
correct: The Lord (by the commandment of the Lord)
explanation: Numbers 9:23: 'At the commandment of the Lord they rested... and at the commandment of the Lord they journeyed'.

question: Did the cloud ever stay for only one night?
opt2: No, always at least a month
explanation: Numbers 9:21 mentions 'And so it was, when the cloud abode from even unto the morning... then they journeyed'.

question: What did the Lord command Moses to make for the calling of the assembly and the journeying of the camps?
explanation: Numbers 10:2 says 'Make thee two trumpets of silver; of a whole piece shalt thou make them'.

question: How were the silver trumpets supposed to be crafted?
opt2: Carved from wood and overlaid with silver
opt3: Cast in a mold of sand
explanation: Numbers 10:2 specifies 'of a whole piece shalt thou make them'.

question: Who was designated to blow the silver trumpets?
opt3: The sons of Aaron, the priests
correct: The sons of Aaron, the priests
explanation: Numbers 10:8 states 'And the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall blow with the trumpets'.

question: What was the signal for the whole assembly to gather at the door of the tabernacle?
opt3: A shout from the camp of Judah
explanation: Numbers 10:3 says 'And when they shall blow with them [both], all the assembly shall assemble themselves to thee'.

question: What happened if only one trumpet was blown?
opt2: The princes, heads of the thousands, gathered to Moses
opt3: The Levites packed up the tabernacle
opt4: The camp of Ephraim set forward
correct: The princes, heads of the thousands, gathered to Moses
explanation: Numbers 10:4 states 'And if they blow but with one trumpet, then the princes, which are heads of the thousands of Israel, shall gather themselves unto thee'.

question: When an alarm was blown the first time, which parts of the camp were to move forward?
